en: Hyperbaric Work - Diving

Daily condition checks must be conducted, and equipment must be examined before use and after each dive. Annual inspections are required, and diving equipment must be inspected by a qualified person. Lightweight gear must be inspected twice yearly.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 28(2), 37(1), 37(3), 39(8)
Equipment Inspections (Before Use, Post-Dive Semi-Annual, Annual)

Inspection records must be maintained for equipment checks and tests. Log retention must preserve surfacing records for at least ten years.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 28(2), 47(2), 50(1)
Records
Diving work must be reported to the inspectorate before work begins.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 29, 47(1)1
Notification to Labor Inspectorate (Pre-Work)
Pre-dive hazard briefing must cover ship traffic, currents, seabed conditions, and known underwater hazards.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 36(1)
Pre-Dive Hazard Briefing
Diving team composition must include at least two divers and two support persons.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 30(1)
Minimum Diving Team Composition (Two Divers and Two Support Persons)

Qualified supervision must be provided by a reliable and competent person. Diving instructions must be given only by persons with diver or signalman training and experience.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 30(1-2)
Qualified Supervision

Signalperson duties must be dedicated solely to the task of communication while the diver is underwater. Signal personnel competence must include occupational safety expertise and knowledge of signals and equipment.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 32(1), 32(3)
Signalperson

Dive team members must be physically and mentally fit, and trained for their roles. Signal personnel must possess competence in occupational safety and knowledge of signals and equipment.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 30(3), 31(1), 32(1), 47(1)1
Training

Standby diver availability must include complete emergency equipment and readiness. Rescue boat availability must be maintained during diving operations.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 30(2), 34(4)
Rescue Boat Availability

Diver minimum age and medical fitness requirements must be met. Divers must be at least 21 years old and medically fit. Annual medical certification must be renewed every 12 months for continued diving work.\nCompressed Air and Diving Work Ordinance, Section 31(1), 31(3)
Medical Fitness Examinations (Annual)
